title: The Footsteps at the Lock
description: The Footsteps at the Lock — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Footsteps at the Lock" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "Accident or Design"
memes.description: "The fundamental ambiguity at the heart of the mystery — whether Derek Burtell's disappearance was a genuine accident or a deliberately engineered event. This question structures the entire investigation, makes every piece of evidence legible in two contradictory ways, and is Knox's most sophisticated narrative technique."

memes.label: Acrasia
memes.description: The philosophical concept of weakness of will or moral incontinence — acting against one's better judgment — which forms the medical diagnosis applied to Derek Burtell by Dr. Simmonds and underpins the Indescribable insurance policy's conditions.

memes.label: "Angela Bredon"
memes.description: "Miles Bredon's wife and indispensable partner in detection. She drives the car, plays patience at his side, adopts the 'river-girl' disguise to gather intelligence among the Thames boating community, and proves that the quietest partner can be the most effective."

memes.label: "Bredon's Cottage"
memes.description: "The country home of Miles and Angela Bredon — a place of domestic peace between cases."

memes.label: Brimley House
memes.description: Residence of Mrs. Coolman at Wallingford, Berkshire, where her protégé Edward Farris served as her secretary and where the contested will was made.

memes.label: "Byworth"
memes.description: "A village on the Thames near Shipcote Lock, significant as a possible origin and escape route for the person responsible for Derek Burtell's death."

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "Colophon — The Footsteps at the Lock"

memes.label: "Cricklade"
memes.description: "The intended destination of the canoe expedition — navigable limit of the Upper Thames, never reached."

memes.label: "Dedication"
memes.description: "Dedication page of The Footsteps at the Lock"

memes.label: "Derek Burtell"
memes.description: "The elder Burtell cousin, whose inherited fortune of fifty thousand pounds and subsequent disappearance on the Thames set the entire mystery in motion. His fate — alive or dead? — is the novel's central question."

memes.label: "Disguise and Undisguise"
memes.description: "The theme of physical and social disguise running through the novel — characters who transform their appearance, and the deeper question of how identity can be hidden in plain sight without any physical disguise at all."

memes.label: "Dr. Simmonds"
memes.description: "The Harley Street physician recommended by the Indescribable to oversee Derek Burtell's health. His prescription of a canoe trip on the Thames sets the entire mystery in motion."

memes.label: "Dr. Tremayne"
memes.description: "The Indescribable Company's medical officer — an eminent practitioner paid handsomely to stop saving lives and instead predict the probabilities of death for insurance purposes."

memes.label: "Eaton Bridge"
memes.description: "A bridge on the upper Thames, about three miles downstream from Shipcote Lock — where the first chapter of the mystery begins to unravel."

memes.label: "Edward Farris"
memes.description: "Edward Farris is Mrs. Coolman's young protégé and alternate heir, named in her will as Derek Burtell's substitute should Derek fail to meet the conditions of the legacy. An orphan from infancy, Farris is physically vigorous but enigmatic in character."

memes.label: "Farris"
memes.description: "The Burtell family solicitor, whose knowledge of the legacy's legal provisions and whose own role in the financial arrangements make him a person of interest in the investigation."

memes.label: "Frontispiece"
memes.description: "Frontispiece of The Footsteps at the Lock"

memes.label: George Lowther
memes.description: A serving-man at the Gudgeon Inn who accompanied Nigel Burtell in the initial search for Derek Burtell, swam out to retrieve the abandoned canoe, and conducted the first dives for the missing man's body.

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "Imprint page of The Footsteps at the Lock"

memes.label: "Indescribable House"
memes.description: "The London headquarters of the Indescribable Company — a vast building whose upper floors remain mysterious."

memes.label: "Inspector Leyland"
memes.description: "The Scotland Yard inspector assigned to investigate Derek Burtell's disappearance. A competent professional whose conventional methods provide a foil for Bredon's more intuitive approach."

memes.label: "Sir John Burtell"
memes.description: "The patriarch of the Burtell family, a noted barrister, whose last will and testament sets the entire plot in motion. His legacy's provisions governing his grandsons' inheritance create the financial stakes that underlie the mystery."

memes.label: "Lechlade"
memes.description: "A town on the upper Thames above Oxford, notable for its distinctive church described by Nigel Burtell as 'a fantasy,' and serving as the upstream limit of the river setting."

memes.label: "List of Illustrations"
memes.description: "List of Illustrations — The Footsteps at the Lock"

memes.label: "London"
memes.description: "The metropolitan setting for the novel's institutional scenes — Indescribable House, Wigpole Street, solicitors' offices."

memes.label: "Luck and Chance"
memes.description: "The role of luck and chance in the novel's plot and in Bredon's detection method — how apparent coincidences are examined, and how the line between luck and design is drawn."

memes.label: "Luke Wallace"
memes.description: "A mysterious stranger who uses the alias 'Luke Wallace' and becomes entangled in the disappearance of Derek Burtell at Shipcote Lock. His true identity is one of the central puzzles of the investigation."

memes.label: "Miles Bredon"
memes.description: "The private detective employed by the Indescribable Company to investigate Derek Burtell's disappearance. A man of cultivated indolence who solves cases through patience and pattern recognition."

memes.label: "Millington Bridge"
memes.description: "A location on the upper Thames above Shipcote, site of an inn where Derek Burtell slept on the night before the canoe incident — a key alibi-related location."

memes.label: "Mr. H. Anderton"
memes.description: "An alias used by one of the figures in the investigation, posing as 'Mr. H. Anderton, a commercial traveller.' The Anderton identity is a fabrication used to establish a presence at a location relevant to the case."

memes.label: "Mr. Burgess"
memes.description: "An employee of the Indescribable Company who provides key information about the Burtell insurance policy and assists Bredon from London."

memes.label: "Mr. Burtell (the Grandfather)"
memes.description: "The deceased patriarch whose will created the fifty-thousand-pound legacy — and the contingent inheritance structure that drove the entire plot."

memes.label: "Mr. Carmichael"
memes.description: "A Fellow at Simon Magus College known for his 'odd ideas.' Mr. Carmichael proposes the soap dish theory about Derek Burtell's disappearance and participates in the Oxford-side investigation led by Bredon."

memes.label: "Mr. Erasmus Quirk"
memes.description: "An American insurance agent who assists Bredon in the investigation. His helpful manner and transatlantic perspective provide both comic relief and crucial insights."

memes.label: "Mr. Quirk"
memes.description: "Mr. Erasmus Quirk is a lodger at the Gudgeon Inn who becomes both a witness and a person of interest in the investigation of Derek Burtell's disappearance. His exact role remains ambiguous: a nervous, garrulous man whose observations and misobservations complicate the case."

memes.label: "Mrs. Burgess"
memes.description: "The wife of the Shipcote Lock lock-keeper, Mr. Burgess. Mrs. Burgess provides domestic hospitality at the lock and becomes a minor but warm presence in the investigation."

memes.label: "Mrs. Coolman"
memes.description: "An elderly woman connected to the Burtell family, whose unexpected legacy provides a new financial angle to the investigation."

memes.label: "Nigel Burtell"
memes.description: "Derek Burtell's younger cousin and contingent heir to the fifty-thousand-pound Burtell legacy. An Oxford undergraduate, amateur photographer, and theatrical aesthete whose perfect alibi at Shipcote Lock makes him the prime object of Miles Bredon's suspicion — and whose mock funeral for his twenty-first birthday becomes grimly prophetic."

memes.label: "Nigel's Mock Funeral"
memes.description: "Nigel Burtell's twenty-first birthday celebration at Oxford — a mock funeral at which he lay on a black catafalque while his friends drank absinthe to his departed youth."

memes.label: "Oxford"
memes.description: "The ancient university city on the River Thames, home to Simon Magus College and the world both Burtell cousins inhabit — one morosely, the other theatrically. Oxford is the starting point of the fatal canoe expedition, the destination of Nigel's claimed train journey, and the institutional backdrop against which the rivalry between the cousins festers into murder."

memes.label: "Oxford University"
memes.description: "The ancient university where both Burtell cousins studied at Simon Magus College — the institution that shaped their rivalry and provided Nigel's alibi."

memes.label: "Patience as Method"
memes.description: "Miles Bredon's distinctive approach to detection — playing the card game Patience while thinking through a case, using the rhythmic dealing of cards as a cognitive tool and a signature personal ritual."

memes.label: "Shipcote Lock"
memes.description: "A lock on the upper Thames between Oxford and Lechlade — a fairy garden of crimson ramblers and stone engineering that becomes the physical fulcrum of the mystery. It is the last place Derek Burtell was seen alive, the scene of the canoe's passage, and the geographical key to the unravelling of Nigel's alibi."

memes.label: "Shipcote Station"
memes.description: "The railway station serving Shipcote, significant in the investigation as a point of arrival and departure used to trace the movements of persons of interest on the day of the disappearance."

memes.label: "Sholto"
memes.description: "An important official of the Indescribable Company and a personal friend of Miles Bredon. He briefs Bredon on the Burtell case in the opening stages of the investigation."

memes.label: "Simon Magus College"
memes.description: "Nigel Burtell's fictional Oxford college — named after the biblical sorcerer, hinting at themes of deception."

memes.label: "Simon Magus College"
memes.description: "The Oxford college of both Burtell cousins — a tolerant institution where 'men cared little what their neighbours did, short of the bagpipes.' Its institutional indifference to Derek's indolence and Nigel's aestheticism is both Oxford's charm and its quiet indictment: the structures that surround us do not prevent tragedy; they merely provide the stage on which it is performed."

memes.label: "Sir John Burtell"
memes.description: "Sir John Burtell, a noted barrister, is the deceased patriarch of the Burtell family. His last will and testament, and its interpretation, drive the central inheritance plot of the novel. He was the father of Captain John Burtell (killed in France) and grandfather of Derek and Nigel Burtell."

memes.label: "Spinnaker Farm"
memes.description: "A farm on the route between Shipcote Lock and the railway station — referenced by the lock-keeper and passed by Nigel on the day of the disappearance."

memes.label: "Suspicion"
memes.description: "The psychological foundation of detective work explored in the novel — how suspicion operates, when it is justified, and Bredon's distinctive principle that the most perfectly alibied suspect is the most suspect of all."

memes.label: "The Alibi"
memes.description: "The central logical puzzle of the investigation — the apparently unbreakable alibis that protect the suspects in Derek Burtell's disappearance, and Miles Bredon's methodical counter-principle that the too-perfect alibi is the most suspicious thing of all. Knox's treatment of the alibi as incrimination rather than exoneration is a sophisticated departure from earlier detective fiction."

memes.label: "The American Agent"
memes.description: "A representative of American insurance interests connected to the Burtell case, whose presence highlights the international financial dimensions of the investigation."

memes.label: "The Arrest of Nigel Burtell"
memes.description: "The arrest of Nigel Burtell on suspicion of conspiracy to defraud — a turning point that shifts the investigation from inquiry to prosecution."

memes.label: "The Blue Cow"
memes.description: "An inn on the Thames above Millington Bridge, where Bredon makes inquiries during the investigation into Derek Burtell's disappearance."

memes.label: "The Body Problem"
memes.description: "The legal and investigative puzzle created by the absence of Derek Burtell's body — no corpse means no certainty about death, no murder charge possible, and no straightforward resolution for the insurance claim."

memes.label: "The Boy Scout Encampment"
memes.description: "The encampment of Boy Scouts on the riverbank downstream of Shipcote Lock — a recurring presence throughout the novel whose members serve as key alibi witnesses, discoverers of physical evidence including the second notecase, and an informal investigative resource cultivated by Miles Bredon."

memes.label: The Boy Scouts Encampment
memes.description: A recurring element in the investigation of Derek Burtell's disappearance — a troop of boy scouts encamped along the Thames near Shipcote Lock, who serve as informal witnesses, salvage divers, and unofficial allies to Miles Bredon.

memes.label: "The Bradshaw Cipher"
memes.description: "A coded message concealed in a railway timetable advertisement, solved by Miles Bredon using the Bradshaw railway guide as a key."

memes.label: "The Bredon Reconstruction"
memes.description: "Miles Bredon's physical reconstruction of the crime on the upper Thames — the canoe journey that proved Nigel Burtell's guilt beyond reasonable doubt."

memes.label: "The Burtell Estate"
memes.description: "The legal and financial structure created by Sir John Burtell's will — the trust that held fifty thousand pounds and set the terms for its distribution."

memes.label: "The Burtell Family"
memes.description: "The genealogical structure of the Burtell family — Sir John Burtell's two sons and two grandsons — whose strained intergenerational dynamics, physical resemblance, and inherited traits form the human substrate of the novel's inheritance plot."

memes.label: "The Burtell Inheritance"
memes.description: "The last will and testament of Sir John Burtell, leaving fifty thousand pounds to Derek — or, if Derek died before twenty-five, to Nigel. The financial motive behind a murder."

memes.label: "The Burtell Legacy"
memes.description: "The fifty-thousand-pound inheritance left by old Mr. Burtell, held in trust for his grandsons Derek and Nigel, which provides the financial motive for the potential crime."

memes.label: "The Camera Evidence"
memes.description: "The camera abandoned at Shipcote Lock and the photographic film it contained, which becomes a crucial piece of evidence — potentially proving Nigel's presence at the scene of the supposed accident, or exonerating him."

memes.label: "The Canoe Accident"
memes.description: "The pivotal event of the novel — Derek Burtell's canoe drifting through the half-open lock gates at Shipcote while Nigel photographed from the bridge. Whether this was a genuine accident or a staged disappearance is the question that defines the entire investigation, and the lock's geography provides the physical key to its eventual resolution."

memes.label: "The Canoe Expedition"
memes.description: "The canoe trip down the Thames proposed by Dr. Simmonds as a health measure for Derek Burtell, which becomes the mechanism of his disappearance and the central mystery of the novel."

memes.label: "The Cipher Correspondence"
memes.description: "The discovery and decipherment of coded messages exchanged between Derek and an accomplice before his disappearance, proving the canoe accident was staged."

memes.label: "The Common-Room Dinner"
memes.description: "A dinner at Simon Magus College, Oxford, where Nigel Burtell faced uncomfortable questions about his cousin's disappearance from his former tutor."

memes.label: "The County Police"
memes.description: "The county police force responsible for investigating Derek Burtell's disappearance — competent but procedurally constrained."

memes.label: "The Death of Derek Burtell"
memes.description: "The disappearance and presumed death of Derek Burtell on the upper Thames — the central crime that drives the entire investigation."

memes.label: "The Disappearance"
memes.description: "The central mystery of the novel — Derek Burtell's vanishing from a drifting canoe on the Thames, and the question of whether he is alive or dead."

memes.label: "The Discovery of the Canoe"
memes.description: "The empty canoe found at Eaton Bridge, three miles below Shipcote Lock — the first physical evidence that something had happened to Derek."

memes.label: "The Dragging of the Weir"
memes.description: "The dragging of the weir near Shipcote Lock — a targeted search for evidence in the turbulent waters where the weir-stream rejoins the main channel."

memes.label: "The Final Reconstruction"
memes.description: "Bredon's complete reconstruction of events — how Derek staged his death, how Nigel assisted, and how the conspiracy unraveled."

memes.label: The Footprints
memes.description: The footprints photographed on the weir bridge at Shipcote Lock — a central piece of physical evidence in the investigation of Derek Burtell's disappearance, whose interpretation drives the reconstruction of the crime by Miles Bredon.

memes.label: "The Footsteps at the Lock"
memes.description: "The Footsteps at the Lock — The Footsteps at the Lock"

relations.label: IV: The Indescribable Has Its Doubts
XII: The Secret of the Island
XIV: The Man in the Punt
XXI: A Walk in the Dark

memes.label: "The Gudgeon Inn"
memes.description: "The inn near Eaton Bridge, run by Mrs. Barley — Bredon's investigative headquarters on the upper Thames."

memes.label: "The Indescribable"
memes.description: "A vast and mysterious insurance agency that employs Miles Bredon as its private detective, willing to underwrite any risk for a consideration."

memes.label: "The Indescribable"
memes.description: "The vast and mysterious insurance agency that underwrote Derek Burtell's life and employed Miles Bredon to investigate his death."

memes.label: "The Island in the Thames"
memes.description: "A small island between Shipcote Lock and Eaton Bridge, searched by Bredon and Leyland for evidence of Derek's fate."

memes.label: "The Lie"
memes.description: "The role of falsehood and deception in the novel — from the possible staged death of Derek Burtell to the web of minor lies that surround the investigation."

memes.label: "The Lock-Keeper"
memes.description: "The keeper of Shipcote Lock, whose testimony about seeing Nigel on the bridge with his camera is a crucial component of Nigel's alibi."

memes.label: "The Man in the Punt"
memes.description: "The mysterious stranger in the punt who passed through Shipcote Lock just before the Burtell cousins on the morning of Derek's disappearance. His identity — later revealed as Edward Farris disguised as Luke Wallace — and his inexplicable vanishing between Shipcote and the next lock upstream form one of the central puzzles of the investigation."

memes.label: "The Moneylenders"
memes.description: "The consortium of moneylenders who lent Derek Burtell money against his expected inheritance — and panicked when he looked likely to die before receiving it."

memes.label: "The Oath and Testimony"
memes.description: "The legal and moral weight of sworn testimony in the investigation — how witnesses' statements are gathered, tested, and challenged, and the obligations created by the oath."

memes.label: "The Oxford Examination"
memes.description: "Nigel Burtell's viva voce examination at Oxford on July 3 — the cornerstone of his alibi that he was in Oxford when Derek disappeared."

memes.label: "The Photograph of the Lock"
memes.description: "Nigel Burtell's photograph of the lock-keeper at Shipcote Lock — a piece of evidence that the camera, which 'never tells the truth,' turned into the key to his undoing. The photographic comparison that exposed the duplicate lock is the novel's most elegant piece of detective work and the turning point of the entire investigation."

memes.label: "The Police Investigation"
memes.description: "The official police investigation into Derek Burtell's disappearance, led by Inspector Leyland of the county constabulary."

memes.label: "The Search of the River"
memes.description: "The systematic search of the upper Thames above Shipcote Lock for Derek Burtell's body, organized by Bredon to prove that a crime had been committed."

memes.label: "The Shipcote Lock-Keeper"
memes.description: "The lock-keeper at Shipcote Lock on the upper Thames. He is a key witness to the events surrounding Derek Burtell's disappearance, having observed the canoe passing through the lock on the day in question. Married to Mrs. Burgess of the Indescribable Company connection."

memes.label: "The Thames"
memes.description: "The river at the heart of the novel's setting, whose upper reaches between Oxford and Shipcote Lock form the backdrop to the mystery of Derek Burtell's disappearance."

memes.label: "The Thames Conservancy"
memes.description: "The public authority responsible for the upper Thames — its locks, weirs, bridges, and towpaths form the physical stage of the mystery. The Conservancy's standard lock design, replicated at multiple sites along the river, provides both the setting for Derek Burtell's disappearance and the architectural clue that ultimately exposes Nigel's fabricated alibi."

memes.label: "The Thames Weir"
memes.description: "The lock and weir system of the Upper Thames — the physical infrastructure that makes the river navigable and, in the novel, makes the disappearance possible. The weir represents the hidden dangers beneath the river's placid surface."

memes.label: "The Towpath"
memes.description: "The riverside path where Nigel ran after the drifting canoe — a crucial element in testing his alibi."

memes.label: The Upper Thames
memes.description: The stretch of the River Thames above Oxford — a secluded, rural waterway extending from Oxford to Lechlade — which serves as the primary geographical setting for the disappearance of Derek Burtell and the subsequent investigation by Miles Bredon.

memes.label: "The Upper Thames Canal"
memes.description: "The canalized section of the Upper Thames whose locks and regulated currents made the disappearance possible."

memes.label: "The Weir Bridge"
memes.description: "The footbridge at Shipcote Lock where Nigel stood with his camera — the vantage point for the disappearance."

memes.label: "The Windrush"
memes.description: "A tributary of the upper Thames, mentioned in the investigation as a geographical boundary delimiting the search area and as a location for leisure activities that reveal character."

memes.label: "Uncle Robert"
memes.description: "A supernumerary member of the Salisbury College Common-room at Oxford and a relative of the Burtell-Bredon family, whose unexpected appearances tend to complicate matters for his relations."

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Uncopyright — The Footsteps at the Lock"

memes.label: Wheathampton
memes.description: A small settlement near the upper Thames with a railway station, used as a waypoint by boy scouts and discussed as a possible escape route in Bredon's reconstruction of the crime.

memes.label: "White Bracton"
memes.description: "A village inland from the Thames, notable for its inn and a crucial letter addressed to Derek Burtell that becomes a key piece of evidence in the investigation."

memes.label: "Messrs. Wickstead"
memes.description: "The Oxford boot-making firm whose meticulous records of customers' foot measurements provide Miles Bredon with a crucial breakthrough in the investigation — revealing that Derek Burtell had hammer-toes, a distinguishing physical characteristic that enables Bredon to identify the real orchestrator of the staged disappearance."

memes.label: "Wigpole Street"
memes.description: "The consulting room where Dr. Simmonds prescribed Derek's river trip — the starting point of the mystery."

memes.label: "Witney"
memes.description: "A market town in Oxfordshire where Luke Wallace stayed on the Sunday night when Derek Burtell was at Millington Bridge, making it a key location in the alibi timeline."
